next_tickets.body: Freeze the S02k handoff atom table and test delayed-peak, broad-late, and low-charge-pair classes against independent forced/random acquisition records plus duplicate-readout charge asymmetry. Expected information gain: separates physically reusable pulse-shape atoms from artifacts before S03/S04/S10 consumers adopt any atom veto or recovery policy.
next_tickets.title: S02l external atom handoff validation against forced/random and duplicate-readout controls

scientific_summary: S02k freezes a run-held-out atom handoff ledger for S02e high-risk timing candidates; ridge wins the ML/NN benchmark, while charge-pair artifacts are separated from provisional pulse-shape atoms.
traditional: fixed S16f/q-template/charge-pair morphology scorecard at 90% train clean acceptance
